questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: 2 diagonals and 2 lines through midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.text: Which shape has infinite lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry because any diameter divides it into two equal halves.

questions.text: What is the order of rotational symmetry of an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle looks the same 3 times in one full rotation (360°), so order is 3.

questions.text: A rectangle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry: one vertical and one horizontal through midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.text: Which of these shapes has 3 lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry, each passing through a vertex and the midpoint of opposite side.

questions.text: If a figure looks the same after a rotation of 90°, what is its order of rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: Order of rotational symmetry = 360° divided by angle of rotation = 360°/90° = 4.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a line of symmetry of a square?

options.b: Line through midpoints of opposite sides
options.c: Line through one vertex and midpoint of opposite side

questions.explanation: A line through one vertex and midpoint of opposite side is not a line of symmetry for a square.

questions.text: What is reflection symmetry?

options.a: When a figure can be rotated to look the same
options.b: When a figure can be folded along a line to match exactly
options.c: When a figure has no symmetry
options.d: When a figure has infinite lines of symmetry

questions.explanation: Reflection symmetry means the figure can be folded along a line (line of symmetry) so both halves match exactly.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a regular pentagon have?
questions.explanation: A regular pentagon has 5 lines of symmetry, each passing through a vertex and midpoint of opposite side.

questions.text: If a figure has rotational symmetry of order 6, what is the angle of rotation?
questions.explanation: Angle of rotation = 360°/order = 360°/6 = 60°.

questions.text: Which shape has both 4 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 4?
questions.explanation: Square has 4 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry order 4.

questions.text: A circle is rotated by 45°. Does it look the same?

options.a: Yes, because it has infinite rotational symmetry
options.b: No, because it has no rotational symmetry

questions.explanation: A circle looks the same after any rotation, so it has infinite rotational symmetry.

questions.text: If a figure has 2 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry order 2, which shape could it be?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry order 2 (180° rotation).

questions.text: Which statement is true about the lines of symmetry of a regular hexagon?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry.

questions.text: What is the minimum number of lines of symmetry a figure must have to be called symmetrical?
questions.explanation: A figure is symmetrical if it has at least one line of symmetry.

questions.text: Which of the following has no line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A scalene triangle has no line of symmetry because all sides and angles are different.

questions.text: If a figure has rotational symmetry of order 1, what does it mean?

options.a: It looks the same only after 360° rotation
options.b: It looks the same after 180° rotation
options.c: It looks the same after 90° rotation

questions.explanation: Order 1 means the figure looks the same only after a full 360° rotation.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a regular octagon have?
questions.explanation: A regular octagon has 8 lines of symmetry.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about rotational symmetry?

options.c: Rotational symmetry means a figure looks the same after some rotation less than 360°
options.d: Rotational symmetry means a figure looks the same only after 360° rotation

questions.explanation: Rotational symmetry means the figure looks the same after rotation by an angle less than 360°.

questions.text: A figure has 3 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry order 3. Which shape is it?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry order 3.

questions.text: A square garden has 4 lines of symmetry. If a gardener wants to plant flowers along each line of symmetry, how many rows of flowers will there be?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry, so the gardener will plant 4 rows of flowers along these lines.

questions.text: A circular fountain has infinite lines of symmetry. If a child spins a toy top with rotational symmetry of order 6, how many times will the top look the same in one full rotation?
questions.explanation: Rotational symmetry of order 6 means the top looks the same 6 times in one full 360° rotation.

questions.text: A rectangular photo frame has 2 lines of symmetry. If you fold the frame along one line of symmetry, what shape will the folded frame resemble?
questions.explanation: Folding along a line of symmetry divides the rectangle into two congruent rectangles.

questions.text: A kite is designed with 2 lines of symmetry. If the kite is rotated by 180°, will it look the same as the original?

options.a: Yes, because it has rotational symmetry of order 2
options.b: No, because it only has reflection symmetry
options.c: Yes, because it has 4 lines of symmetry
options.d: No, because it has no symmetry

questions.explanation: A kite with 2 lines of symmetry has rotational symmetry of order 2, so it looks the same after 180° rotation.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry. If a decorative tile is shaped like an equilateral triangle, how many times can it be rotated within 360° to look exactly the same?
questions.explanation: Equilateral triangle has rotational symmetry of order 3, so it looks the same 3 times in one full rotation.

questions.text: A circular clock face has infinite lines of symmetry. If the minute hand is rotated by 90°, will the clock face look the same?

options.a: Yes, because of infinite lines of symmetry
options.b: No, because the hands change position
options.c: Yes, because of rotational symmetry order 4
options.d: No, because only the hour hand moves

questions.explanation: Although the clock face is symmetric, the position of the hands changes, so it will not look the same after 90° rotation.

questions.text: A square tile is rotated by 45°. Will it look the same as the original tile?

options.a: Yes, because square has rotational symmetry of order 8
options.b: No, because square has rotational symmetry of order 4
options.c: Yes, because square has 4 lines of symmetry
options.d: No, because square has no rotational symmetry

questions.explanation: Square has rotational symmetry of order 4, so it looks the same every 90°, not 45°.

questions.text: A rectangular flag has 2 lines of symmetry. If the flag is folded along one line of symmetry, how many equal parts will it be divided into?
questions.explanation: Folding along one line of symmetry divides the flag into 2 equal parts.

questions.text: A decorative circular plate has infinite lines of symmetry. If it is rotated by 60°, will it look the same?

options.a: Yes, because circle has infinite rotational symmetry
options.b: No, because circle has no rotational symmetry
options.c: Yes, only if it has markings
options.d: No, because 60° is not a factor of 360°

questions.explanation: A circle has infinite lines and rotational symmetry, so it looks the same at any angle of rotation.

questions.text: A triangular warning sign is an equilateral triangle with 3 lines of symmetry. If the sign is rotated by 120°, will it look the same?

options.a: Yes, because it has rotational symmetry of order 3
options.b: No, because it has only 1 line of symmetry
options.c: Yes, because it has 4 lines of symmetry

questions.explanation: Equilateral triangle has rotational symmetry of order 3, so it looks the same after 120° rotation.

questions.text: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 6. If you combine two regular hexagons by joining one side each, what is the total number of lines of symmetry of the combined figure?
questions.explanation: Each regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry. However, when two hexagons are joined side by side, the combined figure is not regular and loses symmetry lines. The figure is no longer symmetrical about any line because the joined sides break symmetry. Hence, the total number of lines of symmetry is 0.

questions.text: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 2. If you draw its diagonals, how many lines of symmetry does the figure formed by the rectangle and its diagonals have?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry (vertical and horizontal). When diagonals are drawn, the figure becomes a square-like shape with 4 lines of symmetry: the two original lines plus the two diagonals. Thus, total lines of symmetry are 4.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle is rotated about its center by 120° three times. How many times does it coincide with its original position during these rotations, including the start?
questions.explanation: The order of rotational symmetry for an equilateral triangle is 3, meaning it coincides with its original position 3 times in a full 360° rotation (including 0°, 120°, 240°). Rotating three times by 120° each gives 0°, 120°, 240°, and 360° (which is the same as 0°). So it coincides 4 times including the start and end positions.

questions.text: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry and infinite order of rotational symmetry. If a chord divides the circle into two unequal arcs, how many lines of symmetry does the circle with this chord have?
questions.explanation: A circle alone has infinite lines of symmetry. However, adding a chord that divides it into two unequal arcs breaks most symmetries. Only the line along the chord acts as a line of symmetry, reflecting one arc onto the other. So, the figure has exactly 1 line of symmetry.

questions.text: A square is rotated about its center by 45°. How many such rotations (each by 45°) will be needed to bring the square back to its original position? What is the order of rotational symmetry of the square?
questions.explanation: A square has rotational symmetry of order 4, meaning it coincides with its original position every 90°. Rotating by 45° means it will take 8 such rotations (8 × 45° = 360°) to return to the original position. Hence, 8 rotations are needed, but the order of rotational symmetry remains 4 (based on 90° rotations).

questions.text: If a regular pentagon has 5 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 5, what is the measure of the smallest angle of rotation that maps the pentagon onto itself? If you rotate it twice by this angle, what is the total angle rotated and does the figure coincide with the original position?
questions.explanation: A regular pentagon's order of rotational symmetry is 5, so the smallest angle of rotation is 360°/5 = 72°. Rotating twice by 72° gives 144°. Since 144° is a multiple of the smallest angle, the figure coincides with the original position after two rotations.

questions.text: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry. If it is rotated about its center, what is the order of rotational symmetry and the angle of rotation?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has rotational symmetry order equal to the number of sides (6). The angle of rotation = 360°/6 = 60°.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle have? If it is rotated by 180°, does it look the same? What is its order of rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry (vertical and horizontal). It looks the same after 180° rotation, so order of rotational symmetry is 2.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle is rotated about its center by 120°. How many times will it coincide with itself in one full rotation? What is the order of rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: The order of rotational symmetry of an equilateral triangle is 3. It coincides with itself 3 times in 360°, at 0°, 120°, and 240°.

questions.text: A shape has 4 lines of symmetry and an order of rotational symmetry of 4. Name the shape and find the angle of rotation.
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry order 4. Angle of rotation = 360°/4 = 90°.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a circle have? What is the order of its rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry and infinite order of rotational symmetry because it looks the same after any rotation.

questions.text: If a shape has 3 lines of symmetry and the angle of rotation is 120°, what is the order of rotational symmetry? Identify the shape.
questions.explanation: Angle of rotation = 360°/order. Given angle 120°, so order = 360°/120° = 3. Shape with 3 lines of symmetry and order 3 is an equilateral triangle.

questions.text: A regular pentagon is rotated about its center. Find the order of rotational symmetry and the angle of rotation.
questions.explanation: Order of rotational symmetry of a regular pentagon is 5. Angle of rotation = 360°/5 = 72°.

questions.text: A shape has 2 lines of symmetry and order of rotational symmetry 2. Name the shape and calculate the angle of rotation.
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry and order 2 rotational symmetry. Angle of rotation = 360°/2 = 180°.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a regular octagon have? What is its order of rotational symmetry and angle of rotation?
questions.explanation: A regular octagon has 8 lines of symmetry. Order of rotational symmetry is 8. Angle of rotation = 360°/8 = 45°.

questions.text: A shape has infinite lines of symmetry. If it is rotated by 15°, will it look the same? What is the order of its rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry and infinite order of rotational symmetry. It looks the same after any rotation including 15°.

questions.case_text: In a traditional Indian festival, Rangoli designs are made at the entrance of homes. One family creates a Rangoli pattern that is a perfect square with colorful symmetrical motifs. They want to understand the symmetry properties of their Rangoli design to explain it to their children.

subparts.text: How many lines of symmetry does the square Rangoli design have?
subparts.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: two diagonals and two lines through midpoints of opposite sides.

subparts.text: If the Rangoli design is rotated around its center, what is the smallest angle of rotation after which it looks the same?
subparts.explanation: The order of rotational symmetry for a square is 4, so the smallest angle of rotation is 360° ÷ 4 = 90°.

subparts.text: What is the order of rotational symmetry of the square Rangoli design?
subparts.explanation: The square has 4-fold rotational symmetry because it matches itself 4 times in a full 360° rotation.

questions.case_text: A school in Mumbai is designing a logo that includes an equilateral triangle and a circle. The triangle is placed inside the circle such that all vertices touch the circle. The school wants to understand the symmetry properties of both shapes to create a balanced design.

subparts.text: How many lines of symmetry does the equilateral triangle in the logo have?
subparts.explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry, each passing through a vertex and the midpoint of the opposite side.

subparts.text: How many lines of symmetry does the circle in the logo have?
subparts.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry because any line through its center divides it into two equal halves.

subparts.text: What is the order of rotational symmetry of the equilateral triangle in the logo?
subparts.explanation: The equilateral triangle has rotational symmetry of order 3, meaning it looks the same 3 times in a full rotation of 360°.
